audit_items.proof_trace: Multiple unchecked return values lead to null pointer dereferences: (1) `RegisterEventSource` at L393 returns NULL on failure, but `hEventLog` is passed directly to `ReportEvent` at L400 without a null guard; the same pattern exists in `lutil_LogStoppedEvent` at L414-416. (2) `malloc(20)` at L395 is not checked before `itoa(slap_debug, Inserts[i], 10)` at L396, allowing a null deref on OOM. (3) `RegisterServiceCtrlHandler` at L424 is not checked before `SetServiceStatus(hlutil_ServiceStatus, ...)` at L437.
audit_items.attack_scenario: If the Windows Event Log service is unavailable or system resources are exhausted, `RegisterEventSource` returns NULL; the unchecked NULL HANDLE is then passed to `ReportEvent`, causing undefined behavior or a crash (DoS) of the OpenLDAP NT service during startup or shutdown.
